California

June 24, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File Nos. I-87-130 and I-87-131 
Dear Mr. Dorsey: 
You have requested informal assistance to provide you with 
written confirmation of your analysis of reporting responsi-
bilities for two of your clients, Sylvia Muise and Howard 
Homan. 
You have specifically advised me by telephone that you 
are seeking informal assistance as you have not sought your 
clients' specific authorization to seek fo.mal written advice.!! 
~. 
Your request for confirmation is a follow-up on· our advice -
letter to you on behalf of Sylvia Muise, No. A-86-248. 
That 
letter is incorporated herein by reference as are your two 
recent letters to me. 
This letter will confirm our telephone conversation as 
related in your two letters. 
You have accurately stated my 
advice to you regarding the proper analysis of your clients' 
respective reporting obligations under these circumstances. 
I trust this satisfactorily responds to your request. 
REL:km 
sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)

Robert Leidigh, Esq. 
Staff Attorney 
April 28, 1987 
Fair Political Practices commission 
P.o. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Re: 
statement of Economic Interests 
Filed by Sylvia Muise 
Dear Mr. Leidigh: 
(213) 626-8484 
CABLE ADDRESS 
RICHWAT 
TELECOPIER {213} 626-0078 
I am sending you this letter to confirm various telephone 
conversations we had at the end of March concerning how 
Councilwoman Muise should disclose contributions to the reception 
held following the memorial service for Leonard Muise. 
Please 
contact me if your recollection is different than mine, as I 
advised Councilwoman Muise to complete her Statement in 
accordance with my understanding of our discussions. 
The determinations we reached are as follows: 
1. 
Councilwoman Muise had made a reasonable attempt under 
the circumstances to discover all persons contributing $50 or 
more toward the reception. 
Mrs. Muise will amend her Statement 
if any other contributors should subsequently be discovered. 
In 
this regard, I informed you that the numbers I have received from 
the various sources I contacted are all consistent and appear to 
be accurate. 
2. 
Councilwoman Muise should disclose all persons who 
contributed, prior to January 1, 1987, $50 or more toward the 
reception if the intent of the donor was to benefit her or her 
family or to honor Leonard Muise. 
As we discussed, it appears to 
me that the intent of all the contributors was to honor Leonard 
Muise. 
As a result, Councilwoman Muise would disclose all 


Robert Leidigh, Esq. 
Apr~l 28." ·1987 
Page 2 
persons and companies contributing $50 or more toward the costs 
of the reception. 
3. 
Councilwoman Muise would disclose Howard Homan as an 
intermediary for the entire cost of the reception. 
Jay's 
Catering will be listed as contributing an amount equal to the 
discount they offered toward the reception. 
4. 
Shirley Brown would be listed as an intermediary for 
the total amount of the sums paid by the Leonard Muise Memorial 
Fund to Jay's Catering. 
Ms. Brown would also be listed 
separately as a donor of the individual contribution she made to 
the Leonard Muise Memorial Fund that exceeded $50. 
5. 
Councilwoman Muise should list the anonymous $100 
contribution provided to the Leonard Muise Memorial Fund and 
indicate on her Statement that she will amend her Statement if 
she ever learns the source of that contribution. 
Thank you for your assistance in helping me determine how to 
advise Councilwoman Muise to complete her Statement of Economic 
Interests. 
SLD:kw 
A027 
cc: 
Howard Homan 
Atj(l:~rs. 
Steven L. Dort~ 
Assistant Cit~ttorney 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
May 1" 
1987 
steven Dorsey 
Richards, Watson & Gershon 
333 South Hope Street, 38th Floor 
Los Angeles, CA 
90071-1469 
Re: 
87-131 
Dear Mr. Dorsey: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on April 30, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Robert Leidigh, an attorney in 
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If your request is for informal assistance, we will 
answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 
18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware 
that your letter and our response are public records which may 
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
cc: 
Sylvia Muise 
Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths

Staff Attorney 
Fair Political Practices commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Re: 
Statement of Economic Interests 
Filed by Howard Homan 
Dear Mr. Leidigh: 
THIRTY-EIGHTH rLOOR 
333 SOUTH HOPE STREET 
lOS ANGELES, CAurORNIA 90071-14eg 
{213j 62e'8484 
CABLE ADDRESS 
RICHWAT 
TELECOPIER (213) 626-0078 
I am writing this letter to confirm our telephone 
conversation at the end of March concerning the Statement of 
Economic Interests filed by Howard Homan, the Director of Parks 
and Recreation of the city of Carson. 
Please contact me if this 
letter does not accurately reflect your understanding of our 
conversation, as I advised Mr. Homan to complete his Statement in 
accordance with that conversation. 
As we discussed, Mr. Homan must list as a donor of a gift 
all persons who contributed $50 or more to the reception 
following the memorial service for Leonard Muise if the intent of 
the donor was to benefit Mr. Homan. 
If the intent was to benefit 
Councilwoman Muise or honor her late husband, the donors would be 
listed instead on Mrs. Muise's statement. 
As I indicated to you, it appears from my understanding of 
the facts and from discussions with various persons who 
contributed to the reception that the intent of the contributors 
was to honor Leonard Muise. 
As a result, I informed you that Mr. 
Homan would not be listing any of the contributors on his 
statement covering the year 1986. 
Jay's Catering has indicated to me that they intend to hold 
Mr. Homan responsible for the outstanding balance for the 


Robert Leidigh, Esq. 
April 28 1 1987 
Page 2 
reception. 
Under the circumstances, any person contributing to 
the cost of the reception during 1987 will be presumed, unless 
there is evidence to the contrary, to be intending to benefit Mr. 
Homan. 
As a result, any such contributors will be listed on Mr. 
Homan's statement of Economic Interests covering 1987. 
Thank you for assisting me in determining how to handle 
disclosure of the reception. 
SLD:kw 
A026 
cc: 
Howard Homan

sections.conclusion: You have accurately stated my advice to you regarding the proper analysis of your clients' respective reporting obligations under these circumstances. I trust this satisfactorily responds to your request.
sections.question_synthetic: How should Councilwoman Sylvia Muise and Howard Homan disclose contributions to a memorial reception for Leonard Muise on their Statements of Economic Interests under the Political Reform Act, including which contributors must be listed and how intermediaries should be identified?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that the attorney's analysis of reporting responsibilities for both clients regarding disclosure of memorial reception contributions is correct, with specific requirements for listing donors, intermediaries, and handling anonymous contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is composite containing: (1) main FPPC response letter dated June 24, 1987 providing informal assistance; (2) attorney's April 28, 1987 letters to FPPC for Sylvia Muise and Howard Homan; and (3) FPPC acknowledgment letters dated May 1, 1987. The June 24 letter is the primary FPPC response confirming telephone advice. Informal assistance disclaimer included per Section 83114 and Regulation 18329(c)(3).

QUESTION: How should Councilwoman Sylvia Muise and Howard Homan disclose contributions to a memorial reception for Leonard Muise on their Statements of Economic Interests under the Political Reform Act, including which contributors must be listed and how intermediaries should be identified?

CONCLUSION: You have accurately stated my advice to you regarding the proper analysis of your clients' respective reporting obligations under these circumstances. I trust this satisfactorily responds to your request.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC informal advice letter confirming the attorney's telephone advice regarding how two clients (Councilwoman Sylvia Muise and Howard Homan) should disclose contributions to a memorial reception on their Statements of Economic Interests. The letter incorporates by reference prior FPPC advice letter A-86-248 and the attorney's two follow-up letters detailing specific disclosure determinations.
